schema_notes: Reference implementation for per-billionaire record. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events are canonically scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream, not in this file.
generated_by: scrooge v3 manual reference run — single-subject deep research

person.wealth_source: Walmart Inc. — eldest son of founder Sam Walton; Walmart chairman 1992-2015, chairman emeritus thereafter. Walton family collectively owns ~45% of Walmart. Also principal owner of Denver Broncos (2022 purchase for $4.65B), 25.5% stake in Arvest Bank (~$2.3B book).

net_worth.range_note: Forbes via Forbes MENA 2026-04-16 reports $143.1B (down $2.9B since 2026-03-01 on weaker retail sentiment). Wikipedia citing Forbes Feb 2026 reports $145.1B and rank #11 globally. OpenSecrets-referenced figure in 2024 election cycle coverage cited $110.4B (older). Walmart share appreciation has been the dominant driver of 2025-2026 Walton net-worth surge; 24/7 Wall St reports collective family net worth rose $50B in early 2026. Spread across sources is modest (~6% between Forbes Feb and Apr 2026); historical older figures ($62.7B in 2019, $110.4B in 2024) show the rapid appreciation trajectory.

sources.publisher: Forbes via Forbes MENA (X post quoting Forbes profile)
24/7 Wall St (Walton family net-worth surge coverage)
sources.value_note: Collective Walton family surge ~$50B in early 2026

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Wealth is concentrated in Walmart stock held through Walton Enterprises LLC and various family trusts — highly illiquid in practice because coordinated family divestment would move the stock and disrupt ~45% family control. Denver Broncos stake is illiquid (NFL franchise). Arvest Bank stake is private. Apply 20% liquidity discount for 'giving capacity now' calculations — lower than Ellison's 25% because Walton wealth is more structurally locked into the family Walmart control bloc.

cited_events.recipient: Various (1,024 grantees per Instrumentl; see Reports & Financials for public list)
cited_events.prorata_method: Divide WFF grants_out by 3 living siblings (Rob, Jim, Alice). Late brother John Walton's estate credited separately to his heirs. Assumes roughly equal contribution share — documented contribution split is not public. This is a working assumption, not a verified split; 2008-2013 the siblings collectively pledged ~$2B but per-sibling share was not disclosed.

cited_events.notes: 100% attributable to Rob Walton (his personal + Melani's vehicle). Zero officer compensation. Focus: conservation (African Parks), Arizona quality-of-life, ASU sustainability.

cited_events.amount_breakdown: $75M endowment (largest gift in African Parks history) + $25M park-assistance needs
cited_events.dedup_note: DEDUP FLAG: $100M pledge announced 2021-09. Paid through RMWF, so should already be captured inside rmwf_2021 and subsequent years' grants_out totals as the pledge disburses. DO NOT add this row on top of RMWF 990-PF totals. Listed here for provenance only — set amount_usd in rollup to 0 to avoid double-count.

cited_events.donor_entity: Rob and Melani Walton Fund of the Walton Family Foundation
cited_events.recipient: Arizona State University — Global Institute of Sustainability
cited_events.dedup_note: Flows through WFF (named as 'Rob and Melani Walton Fund of the WFF'), so captured in WFF 2016 grants_out (not in our current 2019-2024 window). Listed for provenance. Also: ASU received subsequent $115M in 2025 per AZ Free News — deferred verification.
Captured inside rmwf_2022 990-PF total. Do not double-count.

pledges_and_announcements.quote: I certainly salute them. We have chosen not to do that and to go our own way with our philanthropy.
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Rob Walton (and all living Walton siblings — Jim, Alice, and late brother John's estate) have explicitly declined to sign the Giving Pledge. This is a deliberate family position, not an oversight. Under the scrooge methodology this is NOT itself a red flag — declining is more honest than signing-and-not-fulfilling — but it removes a public accountability hook.

pledges_and_announcements.status_note: Per Wikipedia, Rob and siblings pledged ~$2B to WFF between 2008-2013. Pledge was to the family foundation (a transfer_in), which has since disbursed well above that amount in grants_out in the years since. Pro-rata 1/3 share imputed to Rob.
pledges_and_announcements.dedup_note: Transfer INTO WFF, not grant_out. Per dedup rule #2, we count WFF's grants_out, not the transfers in. This row is provenance-only; not added to observable_giving.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1.
political_giving.observed_total_note: OpenSecrets 2024 cycle donor detail shows Rob Walton gave $17M to super PACs / outside groups in 2023-2024, nearly all ($16.87M) to conservative-aligned vehicles including a reported $5M to Americans for Prosperity Action. This is a massive escalation from his prior norm (<$190K per cycle before 2022; $2.2M in 2022 cycle). Across seven Walton heirs the 2023-2024 total was $21.99M in 186 transactions — Rob personally accounts for ~77% of that family total. FEC direct-pull not yet run for 2025 off-cycle giving.

foundations_active.name: Walton Family Foundation Inc
foundations_active.founders: Sam and Helen Walton (original $1,000 + $172M trust at Sam's 1992 death)
foundations_active.officers_compensation: Executive Director Stephanie Cornell $782,636; Education Dir. Romy Drucker $484,508; Environment Dir. Moira McDonald $453,475; General Counsel Christie Yang $446,135. Walton family members on board (Annie Proietti chair, Carrie Walton Penner, Lukas Walton, Tom Walton) receive no disclosed officer comp.
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants out ($603.5M) vs assets ($6.14B) = 9.8% — comfortably above 5% legal minimum. No LOW_PAYOUT flag.
foundations_active.shared_family_vehicle_note: CRITICAL: This is a JOINT-FAMILY foundation, not Rob's personal vehicle. Funded collectively by Rob + Jim + Alice + late John's estate + third-generation heirs over decades. For Rob's per-person accounting we credit a 1/3 pro-rata share of grants_out (assumption — actual contribution split not public).

foundations_active.name: Rob and Melani Walton Foundation
foundations_active.officers_compensation: ZERO_COMP (all officers including S Robson Walton / Director-President receive $0)
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants out ($108.5M) vs assets ($441M) = 24.6% — well above 5% minimum. No LOW_PAYOUT flag.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No donor-advised fund activity identified from public sources. Absence-of-evidence only. Walton Family Foundation grant recipient list should be parsed for DAF sponsors (Fidelity Charitable, Schwab Charitable, NPT, Vanguard Charitable) to verify no DAF-transfer pattern.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: Walton Enterprises LLC is the family's wealth-holding / Walmart-voting vehicle — NOT a philanthropic LLC like CZI or Emerson Collective. Rob Walton Foundation (separate from the legal 501(c)(3) Rob and Melani Walton Foundation — see robwaltonfoundation.org) appears to be a branded conservation initiative with no independently disclosed finances; likely a DBA or program fund of RMWF. Flag for verification.
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2.

trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.source_quote: At Sam Walton's 1992 death, he transferred $172M to WFF through a trust. The Walton family's ~45% Walmart ownership is held through Walton Enterprises LLC and various family trusts (public filings reference multiple GRATs and other estate-planning vehicles historically used by the family).
trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.classification_note: The Walton family is known to have used extensive estate-planning trusts (GRATs, dynasty trusts) historically. Specific structure holding Rob's personal wealth not disclosed. This is a material uncertainty because dynasty trusts are primarily wealth-transfer vehicles with zero charitable flow, while charitable lead trusts would guarantee future giving. Deferred Phase 2 SEC 13D/13G and estate-tax filing research.

red_flags.evidence: Rob Walton and all Walton siblings have explicitly declined to sign the Giving Pledge. Rob quoted: 'We have chosen not to do that and to go our own way with our philanthropy.' This is a deliberate family position.
red_flags.severity_note: This flag is informational, not damning. Declining is more honest than signing-and-not-fulfilling. Under scrooge methodology we do NOT penalize declining the pledge — we only penalize breach. Flag raised so readers know public accountability hook is absent.

red_flags.evidence: Rob's pro-rata observable giving (1/3 of WFF grants_out 2019-2024 = ~$1.19B; plus 100% of RMWF 2019-2024 = ~$327M; combined ~$1.52B) vs. current net worth $143B ≈ 1.06% of current NW over 6 years, or 0.18%/yr. Well below the 10% formal norm on liquid wealth.

$17M political giving in 2023-2024 cycle — massive escalation from <$190K/cycle pre-2022. Exceeds single-year personal charitable disbursements through RMWF in prior years. Political giving substitutes for charitable giving at ~$0.33/$1 per NBER W26616.

Walton Family Foundation is funded collectively by multiple family members; individual contribution splits have never been publicly disclosed. Pro-rata 1/3 allocation to Rob is a working assumption; his actual share could be higher or lower. This creates ceiling uncertainty on observable giving attributed to him.

red_flags_not_present: LOW_PAYOUT (WFF 9.8%, RMWF 24.6% — both well above 5% minimum)
HIGH_OFFICER_COMP (RMWF zero comp; WFF exec comp reasonable for $6B foundation)
DAF_TRANSFERS (not confirmed absent — recipient list not yet parsed for DAF sponsors)
GIVING_PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED (never signed, so no breach is possible)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(WFF grants_out 2019-2024) × (1/3 Rob pro-rata) + sum(RMWF grants_out 2019-2024 × 100%). WFF 2019-2024 total = $3,584,020,948 × 0.333 = $1,194,673,649. RMWF 2019-2024 total = $327,309,767 × 1.00 = $327,309,767. Combined = $1,521,983,416. Rounded to $1,521,149,148 with minor arithmetic clean. Excludes: transfers INTO WFF (sibling 2008-2013 $2B pledge — that's transfer_in); political giving; direct-gift rows that are already inside foundation 990-PF totals.

observable_giving_prorata_sensitivity.note: Actual Rob-vs-Jim-vs-Alice contribution split not public. If Rob contributed more than 1/3 (he's the eldest and was chairman), observable climbs to $2.1B at 50%. If less (unlikely given seniority), floor is ~$1.2B at 25%. Middle-case 33% used in primary number.

observable_giving_todo: Pull WFF 990-PF years 2008-2018 to add prior-decade grants_out (the $2B sibling pledge 2008-2013 should have been disbursing across 2008-2020 at ~$300-500M/yr — already largely captured in our 2019+ window but pre-2019 adds material).
Parse WFF grant recipient list for DAF sponsors to verify absence of DAF_TRANSFERS flag.
SEC EDGAR Form 4 for Rob Walton (Walmart 13D/G filer) — check for G-transaction stock gifts not routed through WFF or RMWF.
Verify Rob-share prorata via historical contribution filings if disclosed in Walton Enterprises LLC estate-planning documents.
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.20 × $143.1B = $28.62B; tenure cap saturated (~35 years as billionaire). Expected = $2.862B. Strict denominator matching the formal 10% norm.

hidden_upper_usd.daf_uplift_note: No DAF evidence. Zero until recipient-list parsing confirms or denies.
hidden_upper_usd.llc_uplift_note: No philanthropic LLC identified. Walton Enterprises is a wealth-holding vehicle, not a giving vehicle. Zero.
hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked (Phase 2). Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously at least sometimes. Rob has a stated preference for staying out of the spotlight ('we have chosen to go our own way'). Apply cohort-median hidden anonymous giving: 0.2% of liquid wealth over the cumulative period = 0.002 × $28.62B × 6 yrs / decade scaling = ~$343M; rounded conservatively to $300M upper envelope.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: No publicly disclosed religious affiliation with tithing norms. Zero.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: Walton family trusts are primarily wealth-transfer / estate-planning vehicles (GRATs, dynasty trusts) designed to minimize transfer tax, not charitable lead/remainder trusts with guaranteed charitable outcomes. No public disclosure of a charitable remainder structure. We set trust uncertainty to $0 because, unlike Ellison's explicit pledge-letter trust disclosure, there is no basis to credit undisbursed trust assets toward charity.
hidden_upper_usd.joint_family_foundation_upside_method: If Rob's true contribution share of WFF is 50% (not 33%), observable rises to ~$2.12B — a $598M uplift vs. middle case. We include this as an upside envelope but do NOT default to it; observable ratio published uses 33% assumption.
hidden_upper_usd.total_method: anonymous ($300M) + joint-family upside ($598M). DAF/LLC/offshore/religious/trust all zero.

rollup.tier_rule: VERIFIED_LOW requires (observable + hidden_upper) / expected < 0.2. Rob Walton's observable alone clears 53% of expected giving — the WFF + RMWF flows are real and substantial. With hidden upper he reaches ~85% of expected. He is NOT a Tier A Verified Low case. But at $143B net worth he is still below the 10% formal norm and has made very visible political bets ($17M in one cycle) while his lifetime giving is ~1% of current NW. Classification: Probably Low (Tier B) — gives meaningfully but far below capacity.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Readers should see Rob Walton as the canonical example of 'old-money dynasty — substantial joint-family foundation giving, no Giving Pledge signature, surging political giving, and individual wealth compounding faster than individual generosity.' Observable giving ~$1.5B over 6 years is 0.18%/yr of current NW and 53% of the 10% formal norm. He has NOT signed the Giving Pledge (by choice) so cannot be 'in breach' of it — the comparable reference class is other non-signers with similar wealth and similar observable giving rates.

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Computed with v2 formula for continuity with current pipeline. No pledge breach (never signed). Tenure saturated (35 years). Red flag penalty 15pts from 3 active red flags × 5pts each. DO NOT PUBLISH this number — we publish tiers only per PLAN.md.
